diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Well-differentiated papillary mesothelioma (WDPM) of the peritoneum
diagnosis.differential_diagnosis: Primary peritoneal carcinoma, metastatic peritoneal carcinomatosis, diffuse malignant mesothelioma, multicystic peritoneal mesothelioma, endosalpingiosis, endometriosis, mesothelial hyperplasia, and reactive conditions
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was confirmed through histology and cytology, which identified WDPM with extensive, intimately associated mesothelial cystic inclusions and zonal calcifications with osseous metaplasia. Immunohistochemistry showed tumor cells positive for calretinin and negative for MOC 31, B72.3, and CK 5/6, supporting the diagnosis of WDPM.
